finances.apps module#

class finances.apps.FinancesConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]#

Bases: AppConfig

name = 'finances'#
ready()[source]#

Override this method in subclasses to run code when Django starts.

finances.models module#

class finances.models.Budget(*args, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: Model

Model representing a budget allocation for a specific category.

user#

The owner of the budget.

Type:

User

category#

The financial category assigned to this budget.

Type:

Category

amount#

The maximum amount allocated for this budget.

Type:

Decimal

start_date#

The beginning of the budget period.

Type:

DateField

end_date#

The expiration of the budget period.

Type:

DateField

exception DoesNotExist#

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned#

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated#

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

alert_threshold#

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

amount#

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

category#

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

category_id#
end_date#

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class finances.models.Category(*args, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: Model

Model representing a financial category (e.g., Food, Transport, Salary).

user#

The user who created the custom category. Null for global categories.

Type:

User

name#

Name of the category.

Type:

str

icon#

Icon identifier for UI display.

Type:

str

is_custom#

Flag to distinguish between system and user-defined categories.

Type:

bool

exception DoesNotExist#

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned#

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated#

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

budget_set#

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

budgeted#

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class finances.models.Transaction(*args, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: Model

Model representing a financial transaction.

user#

The owner of the transaction.

Type:

User

category#

The financial category assigned to this transaction.

Type:

Category

name#

A brief title for the transaction.

Type:

str

amount#

The monetary value of the transaction.

Type:

Decimal

type#

Indicates if it’s an ‘income’ or ‘expense’.

Type:

str

date#

When the transaction occurred.

Type:

DateTime

payment_method#

Method used for the transaction (optional).

Type:

str

description#

Additional details about the transaction (optional).

Type:

str

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

user_id#

finances.signals module#

finances.signals.checkBudgetAmount(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)[source]#

Signal receiver triggered after a Transaction is saved.

If the transaction is a new ‘expense’, this function: 1. Checks if a budget exists for the transaction’s category. 2. Calculates total spending within the current budget period. 3. Compares spending against the ‘alert_threshold’. 4. Generates a Notification if the threshold is met or exceeded.

Parameters:

  • sender (Model) – The model class (Transaction).

  • instance (Transaction) – The actual record being saved.

  • created (bool) – Indicates if the record was newly created.

finances.tests module#

finances.urls module#

finances/urls.py#

URL routing for the Finances module.

All views use Django’s standard render/redirect pattern — no JSON endpoints.

Page views (GET):

/finances/transactions/ → get_transactions_page /finances/budget/ → get_budget_page /finances/reports/ → get_reports_page

Form handlers (POST → redirect):

/finances/transactions/add/ → add_transaction /finances/transactions/delete/<id>/ → delete_transaction /finances/categories/add/ → add_category

finances.views module#

finances/views.py#

Page views and form-handling views for the Finances module.

All views follow the same render/redirect approach used by the dashboard: - Page views: compute context → render template - Form views: process POST → redirect back to the relevant page No JSON responses are used.

finances.views.add_category(request)[source]#

Handle the Add Category form submission.

Creates a new custom Category and, if a budget amount is provided, creates a Budget record covering the current calendar month. Redirects back to the budget page.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – POST with form fields: name, budget (optional monthly amount)

Returns:

Redirects to budget page.

Return type:

HttpResponseRedirect

finances.views.add_transaction(request)[source]#

Handle the Add Transaction form submission.

Reads the posted form fields, creates a Transaction, then redirects back to the transactions page so the user sees the updated list.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – POST with form fields: name, amount, type, category

Returns:

Redirects to transactions page.

Return type:

HttpResponseRedirect

finances.views.delete_transaction(request, transaction_id)[source]#

Delete a transaction owned by the current user.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – POST request (no body required).

  • transaction_id (int) – PK of the transaction to delete.

Returns:

Redirects to transactions page.

Return type:

HttpResponseRedirect

finances.views.get_budget_page(request)[source]#

Render the Budget page.

Computes per-category spending within each budget’s date range, derives summary stats for the stat cards, and builds JSON-safe strings for the Budget vs Actual bar chart canvas data attributes.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – Authenticated GET request.

Returns:

Renders finances/budget.html with context:

  • budget_categories – list of dicts:

{name, budget, spent, remaining, percent} - total_budget, total_spent, total_remaining – floats - spent_percent, remaining_percent – ints (0–100) - current_month – e.g. "May 2026" - budget_chart_labels, budget_chart_budgeted,

budget_chart_spent – JSON strings for the canvas data-attrs

Return type:

HttpResponse

finances.views.get_reports_page(request)[source]#

Render the Reports page.

Computes 6-month income/expense trends and a per-category spending breakdown for the current month, then passes JSON-safe strings as context variables so the template can embed them in canvas data-attrs.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – Authenticated GET request.

Returns:

Renders reports/index.html with context:

  • monthly_labels, monthly_income, monthly_expenses

– JSON strings for the trend chart - category_labels, category_data – JSON strings for the breakdown chart - total_income, total_expenses, net_balance – floats

Return type:

HttpResponse

finances.views.get_transactions_page(request)[source]#

Render the Transactions page.

Fetches all transactions for the user, applies an optional filter query parameter (income / expenses / all), and passes the queryset to the template so the correct rows and filter button are shown.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – Authenticated GET request. Query param filter: "income" | "expenses" | "all"

Returns:

Renders finances/transactions.html with context:

  • transactions – filtered, date-sorted QuerySet

  • active_filter – the current filter value

  • categories – all user categories, for the Add Transaction modal

Return type:

HttpResponse
